title: Understanding Torque
text: Torque is the rotational equivalent of force. It causes an object to rotate around an axis.

title: Formula for Torque
text: The formula for torque (τ) is given by τ=I*\alpha, where I is the moment of inertia and \alpha is the angular acceleration.

title: Finding Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration (\alpha) can be calculated using the formula $$\alpha=\frac{\omega_f-\omega_i}{t}$$, where $$\omega_f$$ is the final angular velocity, $$\omega_i$$ is the initial angular velocity, and $$t$$ is the time taken.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Given that the initial angular velocity $$\omega_i$$ is $$0$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, the final angular velocity $$\omega_f$$ is $$20$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, and the time (t) is $$10$$ s, calculate the angular acceleration (\alpha).

title: Calculate Torque
text: Now that you have the angular acceleration (\alpha) and the moment of inertia (I) is $$4$$ $$kg m^2$$, use the formula τ=I*\alpha to find the torque.
